_meta.notes: Merges into app via loadVocabulary() in voicing_vocabulary.js. Safe to re-run — additive only.

min6.name: Minor 6 (gospel sub for m7 — less static)

dom7sus4.name: Dominant 7 sus4 (quartal open voicing — Maiden Voyage template)

dom7sharp5.name: Dominant 7 sharp 5 (augmented dominant — jazz cadence color)

sus2sharp11.name: Sus2 #11 (Lydian color chord — Thundercat / Rabbot Ho)

7.label: Dominant b9 — tritone tension (omit 5)
7.source: D'Angelo — Brown Sugar (F7 tritone sub for B7)

7.label: 9b13 — dominant with augmented 5th color, Soulquarians texture
7.source: Erykah Badu — Green Eyes (G9b13), Mama's Gun

7.label: Dominant 13 — full extension, omit 5 (Motown/Philly soul color)
7.source: Anderson .Paak / Silk Sonic — G13 in Leave the Door Open

7.label: Dom7sus4 — quartal open dominant, no tritone tension (Hancock/modal jazz)
7.source: Herbie Hancock — Maiden Voyage (Dsus7, Fsus7, Ebsus7), Chameleon

Maj7.label: Maj7 #11 — Lydian sound (omit 5, tritone on top)
Maj7.source: Frank Ocean — White Ferrari / Ivy (Lydian mode center)

m7.label: Minor 13 — omit root + 5, keep b3 (distinguishes from sus4)
m7.source: Leon Thomas / Herbie Hancock jazz-funk tradition

m7.label: Minor 9 — shell voicing (b3, b7, 9) — Leon Thomas signature
m7.source: Leon Thomas — MUTT (Fm7 center), modal neo-soul

m7.label: Minor 11 — omit root + 5, keep b3 (modal float)
Minor major 7 — unusual melancholy color (omit nothing, 4 notes)
m7.source: Thundercat / Mac Miller orbit — chromatic tension voicing

Maj7.label: Maj7 #11 — Lydian sound (omit 5, tritone on top)
Maj7.source: Frank Ocean — White Ferrari / Ivy (Lydian mode center)

Maj7.label: Maj9 — richly extended major (omit 5) — Glasper core palette
Maj7.source: Robert Glasper — Black Radio, jazz-hip-hop crossover

Maj7.label: Maj13 #11 — Lydian upper structure (omit 5, #11 + 13 on top)
Maj7.source: Thundercat — I Love Louis Cole (DMaj13#11 upper-structure sub for F#m11)

dilla.name: J Dilla
dilla.description: Sample-based harmonic composition. Harmony lives in the loop point, not the arrangement. Chords built as fugues of independent melodic lines — bass, inner voice, upper voice each from separate chops, implied chord heard retrospectively. Dorian/Aeolian ambiguity left unresolved. Rhythmic displacement of chord hits creates harmonic ambiguity by timing, not by note choice.

progressions.label: Workinonit — Aeolian VI-VII-i
progressions.notes: Cmaj–Dmaj–Em in E minor (Aeolian). VI-VII-i — descending rock cadence chopped until original context dissolves. Independent Aeolian bassline countermelody against the surface harmony. Tonal ambiguity by omission: surface samples float over with no key obligation.

progressions.label: Two Can Win — relative major/minor float
progressions.notes: Gm–Bbmaj7–Bbmaj–Dm7 in Bb major / G natural minor. Relative major/minor ambiguity never resolved — both readings left open simultaneously. Harmonic rhythm compressed vs. source, feels more jazz-adjacent.

progressions.label: Didn't Cha Know — Bb minor jazz-funk
progressions.notes: Ebm7–Bb7–Dbmaj–Fm7 in Bb minor. Source (Tarika Blue 'Dreamflower') was in Ab minor — pitch-shifted up a whole step to fit vocal range. Bb7 is a dominant that doesn't fully resolve. Extended jazz-funk voicings from the source record preserved.

progressions.label: Fugue voicing — counterpoint as chord
progressions.notes: Chord not played as vertical stack — bass line, inner voice, upper voice each composed independently from separate sample chops. Implied chord heard retrospectively. Ambiguous voicings (suspended 2nds, open 4ths, incomplete 7ths) emerge from sub-phrase chop points, not from explicit voicing choices.

rules: Harmonic content lives in loop point selection — which 4 bars, not how many samples
Leave Dorian/Aeolian ambiguity unresolved — both minor-mode readings open simultaneously
Rhythmic displacement of chord hits extends harmonic duration without changing notes
Chords as fugues: compose bass, inner, and upper voices independently — implied chord emerges
Sub-phrase chop points yield suspended 2nds, open 4ths, incomplete 7ths — use these textures
One structural source per track — additional samples are surface/decorative, not harmonic

thundercat.name: Thundercat
thundercat.description: Jazz-funk bass-as-composer. Diatonic extended chords (min9, maj9, min11) with one non-diatonic wild card — tritone sub or minor-3rd drop. Shell voicings (root+3rd+7th) as foundation. Chromatic approach bass connects roots. Extensions as melody carriers — octave placement is non-negotiable. Dorian default for minor, Lydian color on major.

progressions.label: Unrequited Love — tritone wild card
progressions.notes: Gm9–Cm9–Gm9–Gbmaj7. Three bars diatonic G Dorian then Gbmaj7 — the tritone-down wild card. Gb and G share no common tones; the harmonic jump is the point. Gbmaj7 is the 'knockout card' that defines the Thundercat signature.

progressions.label: Them Changes — chromatic approach chain
progressions.notes: Cbmaj7–Gm7–Abm7–Fm7–Ebm11 in Eb minor. Gm7 is chromatic approach to Abm7 (not diatonic — half-step below). Roman numerals: VI–IIIm–IVm–IIm–Im. Bass walks chromatically between roots. Ebm11 final chord = Dorian tonic with full extension.

progressions.label: Dragonball Durag — ascending diatonic bass
progressions.notes: Gmaj7–Am7–Bmaj7–Csus4–Dm7sus4 over ascending D major scale bass. Bass IS the harmonic motion. Melody uses D major pentatonic; F# and B over A imply A6/9; E and A over Dmaj7 imply Dmaj9. Turnaround: C#m7b5–F#7 (ii-V-I back to Dmaj7).

progressions.label: Rabbot Ho — parallel mMaj7 + Lydian
progressions.notes: Fm/M7–Ebm/M7–Cbsus2#11. Minor/major 7 parallel movement (rare — minor triad + major 7, extreme tension). Cbsus2#11 is the Lydian color exit: #11 an octave above the 5th. Extensions must sit above the 5th or they clash.

rules: Wild card rule: one non-diatonic chord per progression — tritone sub or minor-3rd drop
Shell voicings (root + 3rd + 7th) are the foundation — add one extension note for color
Extensions are melody carriers — octave placement changes everything (13th high = lush, low = harsh)
Chromatic approach bass: connect roots via half-step from above or below
Dorian is the default minor mode — Lydian (#11) used as single-chord color only
Melody-first reharmonization: melody notes must be contained in the chord, key doesn't have to be

herbie.name: Herbie Hancock
herbie.description: Head Hunters / Sextant era quartal harmony. Two-hand poly-chord: LH spread 5th, RH b3–11–b7–9 cluster. Bb Dorian i-IV vamps where the IV is modal color, not dominant function. Rhodes stays in midrange — synth/clavinet covers bass. Dominant sus chords open rather than resolve.

progressions.label: Maiden Voyage — quartal sus sequence
progressions.notes: Dsus7–Fsus7–Ebsus7–C#m11. Each chord is a minor 7th with bass a P4 above the root (quartal stack reading upward). Three of four chords are Dom7sus4 variants. No resolution — all chords are modal color. Template for all Hancock funk-era comping.

progressions.label: Chameleon — Dorian i-IV vamp
progressions.notes: Bbm7–Eb7 in Bb Dorian. Eb7 is NOT a resolving dominant — it is Mixolydian color chord, IV in Dorian. Never resolves. Rhodes stays midrange; ARP Odyssey owns bass. Clavinet strums quartal 4th-interval patterns as rhythmic comping.

progressions.label: Actual Proof — Dorian with Lydian passing
progressions.notes: Bbm7–Eb7–Ebmaj7#11/D. The #11 (Lydian Dominant color) on bar 12 is a voice-leading byproduct of the quartal transposition — not a deliberate modal choice. D bass under Ebmaj7#11 creates polytonal tension before returning to Bbm7.

progressions.label: Am11 poly-chord — quartal voicing
progressions.notes: Am11 as poly-chord: LH = A–E (spread 5th), RH = C–D–G–B (b3–11–b7–9 tight cluster). Full stack: A–E / C–D–G–B. Reads as Am triad LH + Gmaj/2nd inversion RH. Diatonically transposed up a 3rd = Cmaj7#11; further = D7#11 (Lydian Dominant emerges naturally).

rules: Quartal poly-chord: LH spread 5th (root+5), RH tight cluster (b3–11–b7–9)
Dominant chords are modal color — they do not resolve in funk/modal context
Dom7sus4 removes tritone tension — use as groove-locked static color, not cadence
Rhodes stays in midrange (E3–D5) — let synth or bass define the root
Lydian Dominant (#11) appears as voice-leading byproduct, not deliberate modal choice
Dorian IVmaj7 / IV7 is the two-chord engine — same as Wonder/Badu but more open/quartal

wonder.name: Stevie Wonder
wonder.description: Classic period 1972–1976. Dominant 9(sus4) as soft dominant substitute. Dorian two-chord vamps. Abrupt chromatic/half-step modulations — no pivot chord. Gospel IV→I plagal and I7→IV moves. Multiple keyboard layers in studio.

progressions.label: Golden Lady — chromatic modulation
progressions.notes: EbMaj9–Fm9–Gm7–A7–D7 in Eb. #iv7 replaces expected IV — Lydian color. Chorus modulates up a half-step twice in a row (abrupt, no pivot). bIIMaj7 (AbMaj7) in chorus gives the signature 'mysterious lift' before resolving to relative minor.

progressions.label: As — gospel I7 move
progressions.notes: Bmaj7–B7–Emaj7–Amaj7 in B. I7 treats tonic as its own dominant to reach IV (gospel move). bVIIMaj7 (Amaj7) is Mixolydian borrow. Bridge uses bIIIMaj9 (Dmaj9) — chromatic mediant — as gospel lift before return.

progressions.label: Superstition — Dorian two-chord
progressions.notes: Ebm7–Ab7/Eb in Eb Dorian. Ab7/Eb is the IVdominant with tonic in bass — creates forward motion without leaving the Eb center. Entire track is variations on this two-bar Clavinet riff. Groove IS the composition.

progressions.label: Higher Ground — harmonic minor dominant
progressions.notes: Ebm–Gb–Ab–Bb7#5 in Eb minor. Bb7#5 raised 5th (altered dominant) maximizes tension before resolution to tonic. Harmonic minor: the V chord is dominant (not minor v), giving strong leading-tone pull.

rules: 9sus4 replaces bare V7 at cadence — the soft dominant is the Stevie signature
Dorian IVmaj7 (major IV over minor tonic) is the two-chord vamp engine
Modulate by half-step or chromatic third — no pivot chord needed
I7 → IV is a gospel forward-motion device, not a dominant resolution
bIIMaj7 as color chord resolves by half-step or to i — not as Neapolitan dominant
Keep extensions in upper-mid register — 9ths and 13ths above the bass to avoid mud

paak.name: Anderson .Paak / Silk Sonic
paak.description: 70s Philly/Motown soul filtered through a drummer's harmonic instinct. Non-functional extended 7ths as default resting tones. Dominant 13th and quartal sus chords for float. Chromatic mediant key shifts (by 3rd) for structural moves. Short vamps, heavy repetition — the groove carries the harmony.

progressions.label: Leave the Door Open — pseudo-Lydian verse
progressions.notes: Fmaj7–G7/F–Em7–Am7 in C (but tonic C never appears — starts on IV, stays floating). G7/F slash chord gives rootless dominant feel. Pre-chorus modulates up a minor 3rd to Ebmaj7. Final chorus bumps a semitone up — three key centers total.

progressions.label: Blast Off — quartal sus float
progressions.notes: B13sus–G#13sus–Emaj7 in E. Minor-3rd transposition of the sus chord (chromatic mediant). Only one resolution in the song (V13sus→Imaj7) — everything else suspends. VII7sus replaces expected half-diminished; may be unique in pop.

progressions.label: Come Down — P-Funk vamp
progressions.notes: Bbm–Bbm–Gbmaj–Fm in Bb minor. Two bars tonic minor, bVI chromatic-third pivot, minor iv plagal close. Chord change from Bbm to Gbmaj lands on beat 3 with anticipated bass on the 'and' of 2 — syncopated harmonic rhythm is the point.

progressions.label: Skate — chromatic major slides
progressions.notes: Gm7–C7–Am7–Dsus9 in G Dorian. Half-step parallel major slides (B6→C6, Emaj→Fmaj) are P-Funk/Sly Stone vocabulary — no diatonic justification, voice leading smooths it. Descending chromatic major-6th chords in bridge.

rules: Extended 7ths (maj7, min7, dom13) are resting tones — they don't need to resolve
Dominant 13sus replaces plain V7 — avoid leading-tone pull
Chromatic mediant modulation (key shift by 3rd) is the primary structural move
Quartal sus chords (stack 4ths) float rather than pull — use over dominant for irresolution
Short vamps, high repetition — groove creates tension, chords don't need to move
Chord changes anticipate the beat — place on 'and' of 2 or 4, not the downbeat

badu.name: Erykah Badu
badu.description: Soulquarians harmonic language. Cyclic minor extensions (m9, m11), non-diatonic major7 substitutions (bVI, bII chromatic mediants), slash chords as modal ambiguity. Bb Dorian default. Deliberately avoids V→I resolution — loops, never cadences. Gospel major-IV-in-minor lift as color move.

progressions.label: Other Side of the Game — chromatic mediant
progressions.notes: Dbmaj9–Abmaj9–Emaj7–Eb9b13/A in Ab major. The bVI (Emaj7) is non-diatonic chromatic mediant — Badu's most repeated harmonic signature. V arrives as slash chord with augmented color, never resolves cleanly.

progressions.label: Tyrone — Phrygian modal loop
progressions.notes: Bbm9–Dbmaj7–Ebm7–Gbm7. bIVm7 and bVIm7 are Phrygian/parallel-minor borrows. Stays locked on Bbm extended for long stretches — modal static looping.

progressions.label: Window Seat — Dorian vamp
progressions.notes: Bbm11–Ebmaj7–Abmaj9–Bbm11 in Bb Dorian. IVmaj7 (Ebmaj7) is the Dorian sweet spot — major IV in minor context without leaving the mode. Loop gravity is centripetal, not directional.

progressions.label: Green Eyes — jazz-voiced suite
progressions.notes: G9b13–Am/C–B7–Bbmaj7 in G. Fully jazz-voiced: 9b13 dominant, diminished passing, non-diatonic Emaj7 chromatic mediant. Roy Hargrove trumpet clusters stacked 2-3 voices over Poyser's sparse voicings.

rules: Cyclic looping is the default — avoid V→I cadence entirely
Bb Dorian is the primary modal center — minor with raised 6th (Dorian IVmaj7 is the neo-soul sweet spot)
bVI non-diatonic major7 is the signature color move — chromatic mediant, not functional
Slash chords (Abm7/Gb, Bbm9/Eb) create modal ambiguity — read as modal, not as inversions
Cluster voicings: 9th–b3–11th tight in RH, root held by bass (Pino/Poyser split)
Gospel lift: major IV in minor context (Ebmaj7 over Bbm tonic) — color move, not modulation

glasper.name: Robert Glasper
glasper.description: Jazz-hip-hop crossover. Rootless cluster voicings (9–b3–11 close position). Cyclic third-motion progressions — not ii-V-I. Sus chord dominant substitution. Dorian mode default. Avoids functional resolution.

progressions.label: Cyclic minor thirds
progressions.notes: Gm9–Bbmaj7–Ebmaj7–Dbmaj7. Root motion by alternating m3 + M3 — the ic3/4 dual interval cycle. No dominant function. Feels suspended, searching.

progressions.label: Neo-soul loop (Glasper core)
progressions.notes: Bb-11→G-9→Gbmaj13→Eb-11. Cyclic, no resolution, indefinitely loopable. Default Glasper Experiment vamp.

progressions.label: Gospel-inflected minor
progressions.notes: G-9|D-9|A-11|Bbmaj7. Slash voicing on last chord (Fadd2/A). Church harmony + jazz extensions.

progressions.label: Afro Blue (Glasper version)
progressions.notes: Cm–Abmaj7–Ebmaj7–Dbmaj7. Chromatic mediant motion — NOT standard Afro Blue. Each chord a half-step or third away.

rules: Cyclic third motion is the default harmonic engine — not ii-V-I
Replace V7 with 13sus4 — never use a leading-tone dominant in neo-soul context
Rootless cluster voicings: RH plays 9–b3–11 close position, LH holds pedal bass
Default minor mode is Dorian (natural 6th, no leading tone)
Mode mixture as color — slide between parallel major/minor without cadence
Do NOT frame in functional jazz syntax — that is explicitly what Glasper avoids

mac_miller.name: Mac Miller
mac_miller.description: Neo-soul jazz applied to hip-hop. Extended harmony (dom7b9, m9, m11, maj7) over hip-hop rhythmic feel. Modal interchange from parallel minor as primary color tool. Jon Brion film-score influence: unresolved tension, cinematic ambiguity. Swimming + Circles harmonic palette.

progressions.label: Small Worlds — neo-soul ii-V minor
progressions.notes: Fmaj7–Gm7–C7b9 in Bb minor. bVI–bVII–V7b9(i). Jazz ii-V adapted for minor key. C7b9 is the genre's primary tension chord — resolves to Bbm tonic. Key: Bb minor.

progressions.label: Ladders — minor bVI borrow
progressions.notes: Gm7–Cm7–Ebmaj7–Gm in G minor. Textbook neo-soul minor. Ebmaj7 is borrowed bVI from parallel major — Mac's harmonic signature.

progressions.label: Circles — suspension + release
progressions.notes: E–Esus4–D6–A–D7. E major. Sus4 delays resolution; D6 (not Dmaj7 — the 6th is softer) is the Mac Miller color chord. D7 left unresolved — Jon Brion cinematic ambiguity.

progressions.label: Larry Lovestein — jazz ii-V
progressions.notes: Am7–G7–C7–F. Classic jazz ii-V-I in F. Mac's early jazz alter-ego — confirms the jazz DNA predates Swimming by 6 years.

rules: Modal interchange from parallel minor is the primary color move — bVI, bVII, bIII
dom7b9 is the primary tension chord — not plain dom7
Tab sites strip extensions — Gm7 in a tab is likely Gm9 in the actual recording
Rootless voicings common — don't force root in upper register, let bass define it
D6 and Dmaj7 are different colors — major 6th is softer, less jazzy (Circles texture)
Unresolved dominants are intentional — Jon Brion film-score aesthetic, not mistakes

frank_ocean.name: Frank Ocean
frank_ocean.description: Mixolydian bVII borrow as signature move. Modal mixture as atmosphere, not modulation. Avoids dominant resolution — sus chords or modal color replace V7. Sparse harmonic rhythm on Blonde; denser jazz vocab on Channel Orange.

progressions.label: Nikes — Mixolydian bVII borrow
progressions.notes: Dbmaj7–Cm–Eb–Gm in Eb. Flat-VII (Dbmaj7) is Frank's most repeated harmonic move — Mixolydian borrow in a minor context.

progressions.label: Thinkin Bout You — major diatonic verse
progressions.notes: Fmaj7–Dm7–Em7–Am in C major. Textbook diatonic but voiced with maj7 extensions throughout.

progressions.label: Super Rich Kids — jazz altered dominant
progressions.notes: Ebmaj9–Ab13sus4–Dbmaj9–Bb9sus4–Bb7#5b9. Most harmonically advanced track — jazz altered dominant at the turnaround. Hooktheory novelty: 98/100.

progressions.label: Pyramids — minor vamp, modal ambiguity
progressions.notes: Fm–Cm–Ab–Gm in F minor. Aeolian/Dorian ambiguity, never raises 7th. Leon Thomas / D'Angelo shared vocabulary.

progressions.label: Pink + White — four-mode simultaneous mixture
progressions.notes: Cmaj7–Bm7–Amaj7 in A Dorian. Simultaneous modal mixture across 4 modes — most academically documented Frank Ocean progression.

progressions.label: Siegfried — Lydian II major borrow
progressions.notes: Cmaj7–Am9–Em–Dmaj7 in C. Dmaj7 is Lydian-inflected borrow (IImaj7) — unresolved, floating.

progressions.label: Solo — gospel plagal cadence
progressions.notes: Eb–Ab–Eb–Fm. IV-I plagal cadence, direct gospel reference. Same move as Kirk Franklin / Fred Hammond.

rules: bVII major chord (Mixolydian borrow) is the primary color move — use in both major and minor contexts
Avoid raised 7th in dominant context — use 9sus4 or 13sus4 instead of V7
Modal mixture as atmosphere: borrow from parallel modes without announcing it
Sparse harmonic rhythm — hold chords 2x longer than feels right
Extended chords (maj7, min9, maj9) as default voicing — triads only for contrast
Dorian as default minor (raised 6th, no leading tone) — shared with D'Angelo and Leon Thomas
Lydian IImaj7 or bVIImaj7 for unresolved, floating tonic feel

gospel.name: Gospel (Kirk Franklin / Fred Hammond)
gospel.description: Plagal-first harmony: IV-I over V-I. Sus chords as resting states, not passing tension. bVII borrowed from Mixolydian. Harmonic minor V7(b9) for the 'gospel sting'. Extended chords with rootless voicings — omit root, voice 3-5-7-9 in right hand.

progressions.label: I Smile — suspended tonic gospel loop
progressions.notes: C–Csus4–F–Bb–Gsus4 in C. Immediate re-suspension of tonic. bVII (Bb) borrowed from Mixolydian. Gsus4 hangs without resolution — sus as final resting state.

progressions.label: No Weapon — chromatic bass descending chorus
progressions.notes: Abadd9–Eb/G–Fm7–Eb–DbMaj7–Ab/C–Bbm7 in Ab. Bass descends Ab-G-F. DbMaj7 (bVI) is the 'sacred' color chord. Ends on ii without authentic V-I.

progressions.label: Gospel double plagal turnaround
progressions.notes: Bb–F–C in C major. Double plagal: IV is itself approached from its IV. Classic gospel ending. Al Green, Fred Hammond, contemporary worship signature.

progressions.label: Secondary dominant to relative minor
progressions.notes: C–F–E7–Am. E7 = direct V/vi (secondary dominant to Am). Gospel introduces minor vi via its own dominant rather than diatonic iii-vi voice leading.

rules: Plagal cadence (IV-I) is primary resolution — not V-I
Sus4 chords (Vsus4, Isus4) are resting states, not passing tension requiring resolution
bVII (Mixolydian borrow) as a structural color chord — not just a passing chord
V7(b9) for dramatic tension — the 'gospel sting' — resolves to I or deceptively to vi
add9 crunch: voice the 9th between root and 3rd (C-D-E-G) for brightness, not as an upper extension
Rootless voicing standard on tonic: omit root, play 3-5-7-9 in upper register

kendrick.name: Kendrick Lamar (TPAB)
kendrick.description: Minor/Dorian modal funk with jazz extensions. Heavy use of bVI maj7 as a color landing point rather than passing chord. Suspended dominants (ii7/V without resolution). Half-step modulation as structural device. P-Funk one-chord vamp aesthetic from live ensemble vocabulary.

progressions.label: Wesley's Theory — P-Funk Dorian vamp
progressions.notes: Bm7 modal center. Single-chord Dorian vamp, P-Funk tradition. Groove is the movement — harmony is static. Thundercat bass implies 9th/11th as passing tones.

progressions.label: These Walls — parallel major/minor toggle
progressions.notes: Abmaj9–Gm7–Gm7/C–Fmaj7 in F major. Gm7/C is suspended dominant (no resolution). Abmaj9 (bIIImaj9 in F minor) as imported color. F major and F minor sections alternate across the song.

progressions.label: How Much a Dollar Cost — bVI maj7 minor vamp
progressions.notes: Gbmaj7–Fm–Gbmaj7–Ab in Bb minor. bVI maj7 (Gbmaj7) is the most distinctively TPAB color — borrowed major a half-step above the dominant. Polyrhythmic 3/4 against 4/4.

progressions.label: You Ain't Gotta Lie — jazz minor ii-V-I
progressions.notes: Cmaj7–Bm7–Em7–F#7 in B minor. Cmaj7 (bIImaj7) is a Neapolitan-adjacent move, chromatic half-step descent to tonic. F#7 as V7 is one of few TPAB tracks with functional dominant resolution.

rules: bVI maj7 is the signature TPAB color chord — use as a landing point, not a passing chord
Suspended dominants (ii7/V, Vsus4) — imply resolution without delivering it
Half-step modulation as structural device — shift key by a semitone instead of developing harmonically
Minor/Dorian modal vamps — one or two chords, groove-primary, harmony is texture not motion
Parallel major/minor borrowing at the section level — entire sections shift from major to minor
Extended voicings throughout: maj9, m7, min9, min11 as default — triads are intentionally sparse

joe_pass.name: Joe Pass (Virtuoso)
joe_pass.description: Shell voicings (R-3-7) as harmonic anchors. Drop-2 and drop-3 as the primary voicing systems — drop-3 for solo context (bass + 3-note cluster), drop-2 for ensemble. ii-V-I as the central harmonic unit. Tritone substitution on V7, passing diminished between diatonic chords. Voice the melody on top, let inner voices move chromatically.

progressions.label: Drop-3 solo ii-V-I in C
progressions.notes: Dm7–G7–Cmaj7. Drop-3 voicings: Dm7 (D/A-C-F), G7 (G/D-F-B), Cmaj7 (C/G-B-E). Bass note + 3-note upper cluster. Tritone B-F in G7 is the irreducible guide-tone identity.

progressions.label: Tritone substitution ii-V-I
progressions.notes: Dm7–Db7–Cmaj7. Db7 replaces G7 (tritone away). Bass descends by half-step to C — stronger voice leading than authentic V-I. Guide tones swap enharmonically (B/F become F/Cb).

progressions.label: Chromatic passing diminished
progressions.notes: Cmaj7–C#dim7–Dm7. Passing diminished fills the chromatic step. Dim7 is symmetric — one grip covers four enharmonic functions. Bass and inner voice move in half-steps.

progressions.label: Chromatic descending dominant chain
progressions.notes: Ab13sus4–Ab13–G13sus4–G13. Half-step descent with sus4 → resolved dominant per bar. Pass's signature chromatic approach: any chord can be approached from a half-step above or below.

rules: Shell voicing (R-3-7) is the foundation — build from it, not from full chord shapes
Guide tones (3rd and 7th) never move — they define the chord identity; everything else is mobile
Drop-3 for solo/bass context: bass note + 3-note upper cluster (keyboard: LH bass, RH three notes)
Tritone substitution targets V7 only — replace G7 with Db7 in ii-V-I
Passing diminished: between any two diatonic chords, insert a dim7 a half-step below the target
Chromatic approach: approach any target chord from a half-step above or below in parallel motion

ama_lou.name: Ama Lou (UK Alt-Soul)
ama_lou.description: Functional minor with selective extension — closer to Sade's restraint than D'Angelo's modal density. bVI as the signature plateau chord. Harmonic minor V7 for tension points. Dorian/Aeolian toggle without committing to either. Static 2-4 chord loops; melody and vocal development over harmonic stasis.

progressions.label: Tried Up — harmonic minor V with bVI plateau
progressions.notes: Am7–E7–Fmaj7–G7 in A minor. E7 (raised 7th, harmonic minor V) adds tension without jazz complexity. bVI (Fmaj7) as the warm landing. bVII (G7) as passing color before return.

progressions.label: Northside — minor with softened dominant
progressions.notes: Bm–F#–G–F# in B minor. Advanced version: F#m7 replaces F# major — voices the V as minor 7 to soften dominant pull (UK soul move, avoids hard resolution). Gmaj7 adds warmth.

progressions.label: Trust Nobody — Aeolian minor loop
progressions.notes: C#m–B–F#–E in C# minor. i–bVII–IV–bIII: the bVII and bIII are borrowed from Aeolian/parallel major. Most pentatonic-sounding progression in her catalog.

progressions.label: This Town — Aeolian with harmonic V7
progressions.notes: Db–Cm–Fm–C7 in F minor. Cm (minor v) avoids the leading tone — modal Aeolian feel. C7 appears for controlled tension (harmonic minor raise). bVI (Db) as warm opening color.

rules: bVI major (flat-sixth) is the primary color landing — warm, settled, not resolving toward tonic
Harmonic minor V7 for tension — use sparingly as a single controlled point, not throughout
Dorian/Aeolian toggle: minor iv (Fm) vs major IV (F) within the same song for ambiguity
Minor v (Cm instead of C) avoids the leading tone — use for modal Aeolian feel over functional motion
Static loops — 2-4 chords repeated throughout; let the melody and vocals do the developing
Extensions are selective decoration: maj7, min7, and occasional dom7 — no quartal stacks or 11ths/13ths
